name: Brian Dear
headline: Senior Engineer & AI/ML Engineering Leader
tagline: Don't read Brian's resume. Query it.
privacy_note: This MCP server is grounded in Brian Dear's provided career data. It avoids private phone information and does not invent claims. If a question is not answered by the data, it says so and suggests asking Brian directly.
positioning: Founder of iCouch, an early browser-based online therapy platform
Apple software engineer and engineering project manager
Built Rails software for AI/ML human evaluation workflows
Built RAG and internal documentation search tools
Led CI/CD and developer productivity work including a 90% build-time reduction at Apple
Experienced in regulated systems across mental health, insurance, government benefits, and fintech
Comfortable connecting product judgment, architecture, execution, and team leadership
best_fit_roles: Internal tools and developer productivity leader

experience.title: Senior Lead Software Engineer
highlights: Led an engineering and product team creating a greenfield application for the Maryland Paid Family and Medical Leave program.
Managed technical requirements and stakeholder relationships with senior State of Maryland leadership for compliance, customer experience, and financial processes.
Designed scalable architecture integrating Salesforce, NetSuite, and Login.gov.
Worked in modern Ruby, Ruby on Rails, and PostgreSQL while shaping style guides, CI/CD pipelines, and code review practice.

experience.company: Grid Dynamics, contracted to Apple AI/ML Human Evaluation Platform
experience.title: Senior Software Engineer
highlights: Delivered and improved Ruby on Rails software for Apple's AI/ML human evaluation platform supporting high-visibility services including the App Store, Apple Music, Apple TV, Podcasts, and Fitness+.
Improved scoring workflows, performance, and feature delivery for systems serving tens of thousands of simultaneous users in a high-scrutiny production environment.
Built a Rails-based RAG application for documentation search and onboarding.
Contributed Python and Swift where needed for ML-related work.
Built React micro-frontends and data-heavy UX integrated with multiple data sources in support of the Apple Data Platform.

experience.title: Lead Engineering Consultant
highlights: Led architecture and delivery for B2B and B2C Rails applications across multiple client engagements.
Modernized legacy Rails estates at Medical Mutual of Ohio and 3M.
Upgraded frameworks and runtimes, stabilized dependencies, and introduced Kubernetes and blue-green deployment patterns.
Supported Best Buy migrations to AWS and Kubernetes.
Built event-driven systems with Kafka, Snowflake, and RudderStack and delivered integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, and NetSuite.

experience.title: Engineering Manager
highlights: Led roadmap delivery for creditor-facing negotiation operations in a regulated fintech environment.
Managed engineers, mentored growth, and aligned technical plans with business goals.
Built internal APIs, asynchronous services, and third-party integrations with Ruby on Rails, PostgreSQL, and AWS.
Established operational rigor with Datadog dashboards, PagerDuty on-call workflows, recruiting, code review discipline, and post-deployment health metrics.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer IV & Engineering Project Manager
highlights: Led migration of source-control workflows from SVN to GitHub across Final Cut Pro, Logic Pro, iMovie, Clips, and GarageBand.
Built a Kubernetes-based distributed compilation system using a distributed C compiler modified to work with Swift and Objective-C.
Developed custom Ruby-based CI/CD orchestration tooling that cut build times by 90% for creative applications.
Managed build and release operations across iOS, macOS, watchOS, visionOS, and Apple Silicon transition efforts.
Developed ML-driven analytics to predict build failures and improve reliability.

experience.title: Founder & Product Engineering Lead
highlights: Built a mental health startup at the intersection of product, engineering, and care experience.
Founded and built an early browser-based online therapy platform before telehealth was widely accepted.
Created the Ruby on Rails application and integrated WebRTC technologies for secure peer-to-peer video and chat.
Owned product direction, software architecture, customer development, support, sales, and engineering.
The company was later acquired by Modern Health Canada.

experience.title: Lead Contracting Software Engineer
highlights: Led teams of 2 to 25 delivering full-stack Ruby on Rails applications and APIs for clients including Rosetta Stone, Finalsite, Matalan Direct, Sonic Healthcare, and startups.
Established CI/CD pipelines, agile delivery practices, and maintainable Rails conventions.

experience.title: Lead Software Engineer
highlights: Helped scale an HR-tech company from seed stage through Series B.
Built a fully remote team of 15 across engineering, design, and QA.
Architected Ruby on Rails backend systems for synchronous and asynchronous video interviewing and analytics products supporting native iOS and Android apps.

experience.company: The American College of the Mediterranean
experience.title: Adjunct Professor of Economics and Management
highlights: Taught economics and management, adding a teaching and communication dimension to Brian's leadership profile.

projects.name: iCouch
projects.summary: Founded and built an early browser-based online therapy platform in 2010.
projects.context: Mental health care was not yet broadly online. iCouch had to make sensitive patient/provider workflows feel usable, safe, and credible.
contributions: Built scheduling, payments, clinician workflows, and privacy-sensitive patient/provider interactions.
Created the Ruby on Rails application and integrated WebRTC technologies for secure peer-to-peer video and chat.
Handled product direction, software architecture, customer development, support, sales, and engineering.
projects.impact: Helped therapists provide online care before the category became mainstream. iCouch was later acquired by Modern Health Canada.
projects.interview_angle: Brian saw a real human workflow before it was fashionable, built the product, learned the market, and kept trust at the center.

projects.interview_angle: Brian can talk about AI beyond model demos: evals, human review, data quality, internal tooling, and the unglamorous systems that make AI useful.

stories.ownership: Brian's ownership story is iCouch: he did not just write Rails code. He founded the company, built the product, handled customer development, support, sales, product direction, and engineering, and kept the system credible in a trust-critical mental health domain. That is ownership with no convenient boundary line.
stories.ambiguity: At Apple, Brian worked on AI/ML human evaluation tooling where the work was not just 'build a screen.' The problem included scoring workflows, performance, human review, data-heavy UX, and onboarding. He turned a messy AI operations problem into Rails tooling, RAG search, and usable internal workflows.
stories.leadership: Brian's leadership shows up in several modes: managing engineers at Beyond Finance, leading source-control modernization and build infrastructure at Apple, leading a greenfield government benefits platform for Maryland, and building remote teams at Take the Interview. He can lead through architecture, product clarity, and delivery habits.
stories.failure: The career data does not include a detailed failure story. That is a great thing to ask Brian directly in an interview, especially because his founder background should produce a better answer than a rehearsed corporate fable.
stories.ai: Brian's AI story is practical rather than buzzword-heavy. He built Rails software for Apple's AI/ML human evaluation platform, improved scoring workflows, supported high-visibility services, and built a Rails-based RAG application for documentation search and onboarding. He understands evals, human review, internal tools, and the systems around models.
stories.healthcare: Brian founded iCouch in 2010, building browser-based online therapy workflows before telehealth became obvious. He built scheduling, payments, clinician workflows, and secure peer-to-peer video/chat in a privacy-sensitive mental health context. That gives him unusually early product and engineering scar tissue in healthcare.
stories.platform: Brian's platform story is strongest at Apple: he led SVN-to-GitHub modernization across major creative apps, built Ruby CI/CD orchestration, modified distributed compilation infrastructure for Swift and Objective-C, and helped cut build times by 90%. That is platform work with visible leverage.
stories.conflict: The career data does not include a specific conflict story. That is a great interview question for Brian, especially around stakeholder-heavy work at Maryland, Apple, or Beyond Finance.
stories.technical_depth: Brian's technical depth is Rails plus systems leverage: modern Rails, PostgreSQL, internal APIs, async services, CI/CD, Kubernetes, AWS, RAG, React micro-frontends, data-heavy UX, and distributed build infrastructure. He is strongest where product, platform, and practical architecture meet.

role_summaries.rails: Brian is a senior Rails engineer and product/platform leader with deep Ruby on Rails experience across startups, Apple, fintech, government benefits, healthcare, insurance, and enterprise modernization. He has modernized legacy Rails estates, built greenfield Rails products, shipped internal APIs and async services, and used Rails where reliability, integration, and speed all mattered.
role_summaries.ai_platform: Brian is a strong fit for AI platform roles because his AI work is grounded in real tooling: Apple's AI/ML human evaluation platform, Rails-based scoring workflows, RAG documentation search, onboarding tools, React data surfaces, and practical experience with LLM and agentic AI workflows. He understands that AI systems need evals, human review, retrieval, product sense, and boring reliability.
role_summaries.product_engineering: Brian is a product engineering lead who can turn ambiguous workflows into usable software. iCouch, Maryland Paid Family and Medical Leave, Apple internal tools, and Beyond Finance all show the same pattern: understand the domain, shape the roadmap, build the system, and keep users and operations in view.
role_summaries.vp_platform: Brian's VP/platform case is strongest around Apple developer productivity, CI/CD, build infrastructure, source-control modernization, Kubernetes-based distributed compilation, and cross-functional leadership. He has led teams, managed roadmaps, built remote teams, and improved developer velocity in high-stakes environments.
role_summaries.healthcare: Brian has unusually early healthcare and mental health product experience. He founded iCouch in 2010, built privacy-sensitive online therapy workflows, integrated secure peer-to-peer video/chat, handled scheduling and payments, and later worked in regulated environments including insurance, fintech, and government benefits.
role_summaries.startup: Brian is founder-shaped: he built iCouch from concept to working platform, owned product and engineering, handled customers and sales, and later helped Take the Interview scale from seed stage through Series B. He is comfortable where the job is not neatly boxed.
role_summaries.general: Brian Dear is a senior Rails, product, platform, and AI engineering leader with experience across Apple, iCouch, Beyond Finance, Maryland Department of Labor, enterprise Rails consulting, AI/ML human evaluation workflows, RAG, CI/CD, regulated systems, internal tools, and startup leadership. The short version: he can build the product, modernize the platform, lead the team, and explain why it matters.
